positions.category: wti crude oil futures and brent crude futures

driver_clusters: Demand Signals And End Market Pull

beliefs.claim: Near-term sentiment support remains biased upward on fresh EV/battery supply-chain investment and policy support headlines, but it is not yet strong enough to imply a clean breakout regime.
contradicted_by: Supply-addition/expansion narratives (upstream output growth) can cap upside
Chemistry substitution risk narratives (e.g., sodium-ion / solid-state pathway headlines) add medium-term demand-mix uncertainty
Low density of fresh (last-24h) lithium-specific price/stockpile data increases fragility

beliefs.claim: Momentum is weakening because the last 24h signal shape is characterised by isolated bullish spikes rather than a broad, continuously-updated stream of lithium-specific evidence.
drivers: headline-driven impulse rather than sustained confirmation
contradicted_by: If multiple additional high-authority lithium-specific updates arrive within the next 6h, momentum could re-strengthen quickly

beliefs.claim: Reversal risk is elevated to medium because the broader 30d context contains meaningful opposing forces (supply growth + chemistry substitution narratives) even while recent demand/industrial-policy headlines are supportive.
contradicted_by: A clear, fresh lithium supply shock (high-authority, <=6h) would reduce reversal risk and increase directional conviction
A clear, fresh demand shock (e.g., weak EV sales confirmation) would likely flip direction bearish

beliefs.claim: Coffee futures have an upward bias over the next 6–24h as supply-side risk narratives (weather variability / crop disease / origin production uncertainty) remain the dominant fresh driver set.
Freight/logistics risk remains a secondary bullish tailwind (higher delivered costs / flow frictions), supporting a mild risk-premium in coffee pricing.
Demand-side narrative (retail coffee chain activity / functional beverage growth) leans supportive rather than restrictive, reducing downside follow-through risk in the near term.

Over the next 6h, corn futures exhibit a mild upside skew driven by persistent biofuel/ethanol policy tailwinds and input-cost inflation (fertiliser/fuel) acting as a supply/acreage constraint narrative.
contradicted_by: weather: improving precipitation / drought-relief narrative reduces supply-risk premium

beliefs.claim: Over the next 24h, corn futures are more likely to remain rangebound with a small bullish bias unless a clearer weather shift emerges (either renewed drought risk or sustained relief).
contradicted_by: lack of very recent corroborating evidence in the last ~24h increases odds of drift/mean-reversion

beliefs.claim: Near-term reversal risk is elevated to medium because weather signals are directionally ambiguous (drought-risk vs relief) while most demand-policy narratives are persistence-heavy and not freshly accelerating.
contradicted_by: a clear, fresh (<=24h) weather confirmation would reduce contradiction and fragility

beliefs.claim: Near-term uranium price bias remains upward as nuclear policy/technology narratives sustain demand expectations and sector attention.
contradicted_by: Evidence set contains limited explicit near-term bearish catalysts; main risk is headline-driven volatility rather than a clear opposing thesis.

beliefs.claim: Geopolitical/legal supply-chain risk (Kazakhstan/Kazatomprom, Niger/Orano-related issues) supports a persistent risk-premium in uranium.
contradicted_by: If regulatory approvals and mine development headlines dominate, supply-risk premium can compress temporarily.

beliefs.claim: Short-horizon tape is vulnerable to whipsaws from regulatory headlines (e.g., NRC-related triggers), increasing intraday volatility even if the broader bias is bullish.
contradicted_by: If regulatory updates are non-material or quickly clarified, volatility impulse fades and the bullish drift reasserts.
